hook.text: Millions say they believe in God but never attend services. Is religion declining or changing form?

buildup.text: Grace Davie coined believing without belonging to describe Europe's pattern of private faith, no ritual.

discovery.text: People still pray, hold spiritual beliefs, and mark life events religiously — just without regular attendance.

twist.text: The reverse exists too: belonging without believing. Some attend church for community, not conviction.

climax.text: Measuring religion by attendance alone misses its persistence in private life and cultural identity.

punchline.text: Empty pews don't mean empty belief.
